content.claim: Ethnic Roma segregation in Hungarian schools has significantly increased since 1989 and is distinct from socioeconomic segregation; Roma students are concentrated in weaker tracks system-wide and less likely to attend academic tracks or continue secondary schooling.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Segregation index based on 1980 and 2011 data; intermediate trends not fully documented
Causal mechanisms linking prejudice, school choice, and track enrollment are inferred rather than directly measured
Non-Roma parental motivation may reflect perceived school quality independent of Roma presence
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
